name: Sun
title: The Sun: Our Solar System's Star
about: The Sun is the heart of our solar system, providing light, heat, and energy to every planet. Its gravitational pull keeps the planets in orbit, while nuclear fusion inside its core produces enormous amounts of energy.

name: Mercury
title: Mercury: The Closest Planet
about: Mercury is the smallest planet and the closest to the Sun. It has a rocky surface filled with craters and experiences extreme temperatures between day and night.

name: Venus
title: Venus: Earth's Toxic Twin
about: Venus is similar in size to Earth but has a thick atmosphere of carbon dioxide that traps heat, making it the hottest planet in the solar system.

name: Earth
title: Earth: Our Blue Marble
about: Earth is the only known planet that supports life. It contains liquid water, a breathable atmosphere, and ideal conditions for living organisms.

name: Mars
title: Mars: The Red Planet
about: Mars is known for its reddish appearance caused by iron oxide. Scientists continue exploring Mars to search for signs of ancient life.

name: Jupiter
title: Jupiter: The Gas Giant
about: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. It is famous for the Great Red Spot and its many moons.

name: Saturn
title: Saturn: The Ringed Planet
about: Saturn is well known for its beautiful ring system. It is a gas giant with dozens of moons orbiting around it.

name: Uranus
title: Uranus: The Tilted Planet
about: Uranus rotates on its side, making it unique among the planets. Its atmosphere contains hydrogen, helium, and methane.

name: Neptune
title: Neptune: The Distant World
about: Neptune is the farthest planet from the Sun. It is an ice giant with powerful winds and a deep blue appearance.
